content: My daily routine is pretty packed these days. Between work at Goldman Sachs and renovating our new house in Salt Lake City, I barely have time for myself.
Renovating a house while working in finance sounds intense! What's your typical weekday look like?
I'm up by 5:30 to get in a quick mountain bike ride before work. The trails near our house are amazing - I can be out for an hour and back before 7.
That's impressive dedication! Do you bike year-round or does the Utah weather slow you down?
I try to, but winter's tough. That's when I switch to my 3D printer - I've been making all sorts of gadgets for the house renovation. Built custom brackets for the kitchen cabinets last week.
A 3D printer is so useful for renovations! What else have you printed? I'd love to see some of your projects.
Mostly practical stuff - cable organizers, replacement parts for old fixtures. I'm also into home brewing, so I printed a temperature controller for my fermenter.
That's clever! Do you brew beer often? What's your favorite style to make?
IPAs mostly. I'm trying to perfect a hazy recipe. Takes a lot of trial and error, which fits my personality - I'm pretty detail-oriented from my finance work.
The precision from finance probably helps with brewing science! How do you unwind after a long day of numbers and renovations?
Usually I'll read sci-fi novels for an hour before bed. Right now I'm reading 'Project Hail Mary' - it's fantastic. Helps me stop thinking about mortgage calculations.
I love that book! The science is so creative. Do you have a dedicated reading spot in your new house?
Yeah, I converted part of the basement into a little library/man-cave. Got a comfy chair, good lighting, and shelves for my books and board games.
That sounds like the perfect retreat! Have you had friends over to see the renovations yet?
